lines: Susanna Fontana draws a line through one household entry before checking the tap. She restores the name in smaller figures.
"If you need passage, wait. If you came about the water, stand where I can hear you."

choices.label: Ask what the water court needs

questUpdate.log: Susanna asks you to inspect the throttled condenser with Noa Faber.

choices.label: Report that every offer is known

questUpdate.log: You have heard South Measure’s available terms. Trace the buried pulse through the closed work areas.

lines: Susanna holds the pencil above the next blank line.

choices.label: Ask what a Censure writ changes
Ask about the Compact and the Chain
Tell her the arrival fires are missing from the water claim
Ask about a resident isolation plan

lines: "The condenser can carry full pressure. Noa cut it back after the pipe began answering each water cycle."
"Aurelia can monitor the full flow. Darius can cover a ration with cistern wagons. Cassian hears something beneath the pipes that I would rather not ignore."

choices.label: Offer to trace a resident isolation route
Ask why the north lane stays closed

lines: "Your writ gets your boots through. It does not fill the last row’s cups."
"I can clear one body from the lane. I will not clear the queue so the road can pretend we were never here."
"Aurelia opens individual doors while Darius keeps freight moving. Each asks South Measure to become easier to divide."
"Hear them, then bring me the exact cost. I have no use for a generous adjective."
Susanna turns the board and finds an unused margin.
"Fire Seven arrived after yesterday’s count, which makes this an error instead of a verdict. Tell Aaron the next carrier leaves with eleven cups."
"Noa thinks the old relief return can carry a smaller local loop if the north feed is cut. The valve order should be in the annex or below the admission booth."
"Do not promise me clean water before you trace the buried answer. Put the route on the table and let the town see who works it."
Susanna writes MONITORING, CISTERNS, and LOCAL LOOP on the back of the water roll. FEED CLOSED goes beneath them.
"Good. No one gets to become our only answer by speaking first."
"Those are the terms we can weigh here. Take my civil key below the admission booth. The other doors answer to the people using them."

choices.label: Leave the offers on the board

lines: "I would choose the plan that leaves us able to say no next winter."
"That may not be the plan that fills every cup tonight. Steward is the name for carrying both facts without dropping one."

choices.label: Leave Susanna to the next household

lines: Susanna checks the first post-assembly water cycle against the signed abstract.
"The chain is up. I am still here because decisions need witnesses after the speeches end."
"Aurelia holds a pump key. The water schedule remains the one the assembly chose."
"Aurelia has signed the consent limits we won. The next assessor may test whether ink can be tired out."
"Darius’s clerk copied the pump key into the note before noon. He wanted the entry made twice."
"The assembly chose its water plan separately. We will count any debt that tries to cross between the two."
"The keys are ours. So is the midnight valve watch when the west bearing complains."
"Freedom that leaves the maintenance to someone else is only a prettier contract."
"No outside hand reaches the pump. No household gets to pretend the smaller cup is an accident."
"The ration board will make enemies honestly. That is the narrow virtue available."
